statements.__comment: Flag running VMs without QEMU guest agent enabled
statements.query: MATCH (v:ProxmoxVM {cluster_id: $CLUSTER_ID}) WHERE v.template = false AND v.status = 'running' AND (v.agent_enabled IS NULL OR v.agent_enabled = false) SET v.agent_risk = true
statements.name: Mark running VMs without guest agent

statements.__comment: Flag VMs with guest agent but no data reported (agent not running inside VM)
statements.query: MATCH (v:ProxmoxVM {cluster_id: $CLUSTER_ID}) WHERE v.template = false AND v.agent_enabled = true AND (v.guest_hostname IS NULL OR v.guest_hostname = '') SET v.agent_risk = true
statements.name: Mark VMs with guest agent enabled but not responding

statements.__comment: Flag VMs with outdated guest OS (no OS info from agent)
statements.query: MATCH (v:ProxmoxVM {cluster_id: $CLUSTER_ID}) WHERE v.template = false AND v.agent_enabled = true AND (v.guest_os_name IS NULL OR v.guest_os_name = '') SET v.os_risk = true
statements.name: Mark VMs without guest OS info

statements.__comment: Flag VMs with deprecated/unsupported guest OS versions
statements.query: MATCH (v:ProxmoxVM {cluster_id: $CLUSTER_ID}) WHERE v.guest_os_name IN ['windows 7', 'windows 8', 'windows 2008', 'windows 2012', 'centos 7', 'ubuntu 16.04', 'ubuntu 18.04', 'debian 9', 'debian 10'] SET v.os_risk = true
statements.name: Mark VMs with EOL guest OS
